message:
chore: add 85 new beads for spec sections §3, §15, §19 and subtasks

Add 85 new issue tracking entries (62 tasks, 24 epics, no modifications
to existing beads) covering three major spec sections and their
decomposed implementation subtasks:

§3 RaptorQ — The Information-Theoretic Foundation (bd-1hi + 33 subtasks):
  - GF(256) arithmetic verification suite (§3.2.1)
  - RaptorQ symbol operations (§3.2.2)
  - WAL repair symbol generation and recovery (§3.4.1)
  - Fountain-coded replication sender/receiver (§3.4.2)
  - Fountain-coded snapshot shipping (§3.4.3)
  - MVCC version chain XOR delta compression (§3.4.4)
  - GF(256) patch algebra and merge safety rules (§3.4.5)
  - Erasure-coded page storage .db-fec sidecar (§3.4.6)
  - ECS-native replication architecture (§3.4.7)
  - ECS substrate: ObjectId, SymbolRecord, deterministic repair,
    commit marker stream, symbol record logs, RootManifest,
    inter-object coding, compaction, tiered storage (§3.5.x)
  - RaptorQ permeation map, decode proofs, deterministic encoding,
    symbol size policy, boldness constraint (§3.6.x)

§15 Exclusions — What We Are NOT Building (bd-177):
  - Boundary document defining what is out of scope with rationale
  - Blocks bd-1wx (Phase 2 completion tracking)

§19 C SQLite Behavioral Reference (bd-13r):
  - Reference table mapping C SQLite source files to behavioral specs
  - Used for spec extraction and conformance testing

Additional epics and tasks for Phase 2 completion tracking (bd-1wx),
spec section decomposition (bd-22n series for §3.5 ECS substrate,
bd-3go series for §3.6 integration constraints, bd-iwu series for
§3.4 applications), and cross-cutting implementation tasks.

All new beads have appropriate labels, priorities, and dependency
relationships wired via br dep add.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
